75957.subject: 🟪 The machines that wait
75957.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9f199753-ca31-4e3d-9435-5e12896ecff5/BD_NL_Banner_Heading.png?t=1781793903)
Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e60d7cde-4250-41f8-9443-28ed51b80560/divider_transparent.png?t=1774649182)
Caption: 

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3bd8a492-ff61-41db-a7f2-c821d481fd72/BD_NL_QuoteCircle.png?t=1781794162)
Caption: 

“Precisely because we cannot predict the moment, we must be ready at all moments.”
_— __CS Lewis_



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e727c9fc-b6f3-4f8b-8f03-a9cc31a66914/image.png?t=1784064825)
Caption: 

# The machines that wait

Modern police cars are packed with electronics, including a rugged laptop connected to dash-mounted cameras, automatic license plate readers, radios, positioning systems, and law-enforcement databases.

At the start of a shift, those computers can take several minutes to boot up — so they're not shut down until the end of the shift. 

It’s the main reason police officers keep their cars running, even when they’re not obviously doing anything. No one wants their first responders responding several minutes late because their laptop had to be restarted. 

This is how data centers work, too.

Even when idle, data centers leave their thousands of servers running — processors awake, memory live, network connections active — because waking them up to respond to your prompt takes too long. 

No one wants to wait several seconds to find out whether tomatoes are really a fruit.

As a result, data centers burn electricity just waiting for prompts to arrive. A lot of it. 

Because they spend _a lot_ of time waiting.

A recent [study](https://cast.ai/reports/kubernetes-optimization-report/) by Cast AI reports that the GPUs housed in data centers spend just 5% of their time doing useful work.

_5%!!_

Yes, these are the same data centers that hyperscalers can’t build fast enough, packed with the GPUs that Nvidia can’t make fast enough, delivering the AI people can’t seem to get enough of.

But most of them go unused, most of the time.

Generative AI is a bizarro market where hyperscalers and chip makers continuously raise prices for products and services their customers hardly ever use.

Cast AI blames this primarily on hoarding. 

High-end GPUs are scarce and their procurement has long lead times, their report explains. As a result, organizations are forced to guess at their future demand, signing contracts well in advance of delivery. 

They generally overguess, preferring to pay extra rather than be caught short of compute. 

Worse, even wh

75956.subject:
Warren Buffett Cuts Gates Foundation From Donations For First Time
 Since 2006
75956.body:
Forbes P.M.

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/warren-buffett-cuts-gates-foundation-from-donations-first-gssec?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bhf%2FCq%2B7ZSKiBB9t66GTEEQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1Lc42aMYk4JIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrl5oa8o~su-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrl5oa8o-su
  
Good evening, Warren Buffett excluded the Gates Foundation from his midyear donations for the first time since 2006 after donating billions last summer. These plans were first reported by the Wall Street Journal in June, which said Buffett was delaying a decision until he could see the results of a review of the Gates Foundation’s ties to disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein. Here’s what else to know. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Jeffrey Greenberg/Universal Images Group via Getty Images Taco Bell Investigated In Multistate Cyclosporiasis Outbreak, Report Says As the gastrointestinal illness has sickened thousands across the country, some locations have pulled ingredients as a precaution. Jamel Toppin for Forbes The Backlash Against AI Car Surveillance Company Flock Safety The LAPD said it wouldn’t work with the $7.5 billion startup until it had privacy rules in place related to the data that 138 Flock cameras across the jurisdiction were collecting.
Приєднатися до обговорення
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/warren-buffett-cuts-gates-foundation-from-donations-first-gssec?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bhf%2FCq%2B7ZSKiBB9t66GTEEQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1Lc42aMYk4JIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-fz96fz~mrl5oa8o~su-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrl5oa8o-su

75951.subject: 25 AI Prompts That Actually Work
75951.body:
The AI Evolution Newsletter

Автор: AI Evolution


Автор: AI Evolution

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/25-ai-prompts-actually-work-ai-evolution-1k0yc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BTKtbwWnASc6JuCVQNXnWlA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0R2-Pyb4pyJsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrkzzvvc~4c-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrkzzvvc-4c
  
Most people use AI wrong for content. They copy-paste a prompt, get generic output, then spend 30 minutes editing it to not sound like AI. We've been testing prompts for months. Kept the ones that actually work. 25 of them. 5 categories: LinkedIn posts, emails, landing pages, content repurposing, and research. Each one includes the exact prompt, why it works, a real example, and variations to test. No fluff. No filler. Just the prompts we actually use. Grab it free: https://www.chasedimond.com/25-ai-prompts-that-actually-work
Приєднатися до обговорення

75950.subject: 🔷 The Daily: UK defers crypto tax on DeFi lending
75950.body:
US government moves over $288 million in seized bitcoin, ether to Coinbase Prime: Arkham | US banking groups urge Senate to strengthen stablecoin provisions in Clarity Act | CleanSpark signs $6.6 billion, 20-year data center lease with unnamed global tech firm | European Central Bank taps 36 payment providers for digital euro pilot |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Daniel Kuhn

Happy Tuesday! *Bitcoin* gained after a softer-than-expected U.S. inflation print, with analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408263/soft-print-hard-regime-bitcoin-climbs-64000-june-cpi-falls-largest-monthly-drop-since-2020?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) at *Sygnum* , *21Shares* , and *Wintermute* pointing to easing rate-hike expectations, improving liquidity conditions, and a possible break above $66,000.

In today's newsletter, *HMRC* adopts "no gain, no loss" tax treatment for crypto lending, *U.S. government-linked wallets* move millions of dollars in seized crypto to *Coinbase Prime* , *U.S. banking groups* urge the *Senate* to strengthen the *Clarity Act's* stablecoin provisions, and more.

Meanwhile, decentralized AI project *Nous Research* is reportedly in talks to raise ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408237/decentralized-ai-project-nous-research-in-talks-to-raise-75m-at-1-5b-valuation-report?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) $75 million at a $1.5 billion valuation.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🇬🇧 UK HMRC adopts 'no gain, no loss' tax treatment for crypto lending, liquidity pools*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/408206/uk-hmrc-adopts-no-gain-no-loss-tax-treatment-for-crypto-lending-liquidity-pools?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The UK's *HM Revenue & Customs* will treat certain crypto lending and liquidity pool disposals as "no gain, no loss," deferring Capital Gains Tax until users make an economic disposal of the underlying crypto.

* The measure takes effect April 6, 2027, and is expected to affect around 700,000 individuals who use crypto loan and liquidity pool arrangements.
* The rules cover three scenarios: lending arrangements swapped for the same type of crypto; borrowing arrangements, where borrowed crypto is valued at market rate and collateral is disregarded; and liquidity pool entries via automated market makers.
* Any difference
